string.text: Hey, Valyukha...\nWeren't you collecting something for Sidorovich?
Hey. Well, I was. Why? \nAre you heading there? Could you take it, maybe? \nI really can't get away right now.
No need to rush anymore. \nThey say he died tonight.
Who?! Sidorovich?! Get out of here! \nHoly shit... What from?
I don't know. \nI just got a message from a guy at the Cordon: \n"Sidorovich died tonight, the funeral is on Monday at 10 AM at the Cordon."
Well I'll be... And what now? \nWho do we take our loot to?
I don't know. \nBut someone will definitely be there. \nA holy place... And all that.
You're full of it!.. I asked around here. \nLook:
To everyone! I am alive and well, \nand wasn't planning on dying. \nThe joker has already been tracked down and punished. Sidorovich...
Ha! See that?
Yeah... What bastards. \nNothing is sacred...
Sidorovich!?
Yeah?
When are you sending letters outside \nnext time?
The day after tomorrow. Then on the 27th.
How much?
Do you have just a letter? \nOr a package?
No, a letter. Back home.
Same as usual. Bring it over, we'll make a deal. \nIt'll be cheaper if you wait until the 27th.
If I give it to the guys at the Garbage today, \nthey should be coming to see you, - will it go out the day after tomorrow?
What am I to you, Federal Express? \nHow should I know... I'll try...
Don't seal the letter, \nput the money inside. Keep in mind, I read the letters \nand, if necessary, cross out anything unnecessary...
And don't put anything extra \nin the envelope, besides the money. \nOnly the letter goes outside the perimeter, everything else stays with me.
Yeah, I know, I just sent one recently.
Well then bring it today, \nI'll try to get it out the morning of the day after tomorrow.
Thanks!
Lyokha! We're going to Agroprom tomorrow. \nThe military has rockets for flare guns, we need to grab about ten... \nNo, better make it twenty.
Right, I'll run there right now. \nWhat do you need rockets for?
Duty threw some work our way. \nVoronin's birthday is coming up soon. That's why they're asking to bring them. For a fireworks show...
I agreed. Want to come with me? \nThey pay well.
They might pay well, \nbut the military shoots well too... \nAnd by the way, they don't shoot flares into the air, they shoot high-caliber rounds from APCs...
And I have my doubts... \nLooks like you just want to stock up under this excuse. \nThey need us there with flare guns like they need last year's snow.
Come on, we're not going there to steal! \nI've been messaging a guy there, \nfor vodka he'll set you up with whatever you want, even a Topol missile.
No, I'm not going. And I don't advise you to either. \nThey'll shoot you, and more importantly - they'll take the vodka.
What a coward you are, huh! \nWhy the hell did you even come to the Zone? You should have stayed home...
None of your business. \nAnd don't offer me any of your crap anymore.
Mityai, you write poetry, right? \nHey.
Well, sometimes... \nHey.
I need something about the Zone, \nbut for a girl, you know?
What's there to know? \nWhere did you even find a girl, tell me, maybe I'll...
Alright, long story short:
"The Zone, it's scary, death is at every step, \nbut I love you and only think about you, even in my most deadly hour".
Will that work?
Yeah, something like that. \nHow did you figure it out?
I just recently \nwrote exactly that kind of thing. Here, look - from my latest:

We lit a fire near the village. 
Resin like a tear on the logs, 
I started a song with my guitar 
About your smile and your eyes.

You are far, far away right now, 
There are checkpoints standing between us, 
It's not easy for me to reach you, 
As long as I have bolts in my pocket.
That's an excerpt. Will it do?
Yeah, it's exactly what I need! \nWill you give me the whole poem?
Four thousand or a Flash.
You're asking way too much!
Then come up with it yourself!
Alright, fine, I'll think about it, \nI'll get back to you later.
Sakharov is a bastard!!!
Hey. What's wrong with you? \nWhat did he do to you?
I agreed with the professor, \nthat he'd give me a good job. \nI went to see him, and there's already some Duty member sitting there...
Sakharov says, sorry, \nbuddy, you're late, it's already done. \nAnd they're both smirking. Especially the Duty member, arrogant mug!
Well, what did you expect? \nIt's clear as day: whoever got there first got the job. \nSakharov favors Duty members.
That's not how you do business. \nWe had a deal!
Come on, let it go. \nCome with me to the Garbage the day after tomorrow, \nBes needs something there: he's gathering a large group.
To take out bandits, or what? \nIs Bes definitely there? I heard he was leaving...
No, probably not bandits, \nit's quiet there right now. But he needs something...
Does it even matter to you, \nBes or no Bes? We're out of money. \nAnd there's work there, and people to chat with.
All you ever do is chat... \nAlright, let's go. In the morning?
Yeah. Are you at the Bar right now? \nI'll find you there tomorrow at eight. \nWe'll get to the Garbage right around noon.
It's a deal, I'll be waiting.
Hey! We need groceries. \nHow are things looking with that right now?
And same to you! \nThings are not looking great. What exactly do you need?
Something non-perishable. \nCanned goods, mostly. Dry rations. Alcohol.
Going far?
Well, for three or four days. \nThere are five of us. Plus there will be seven more there, on site. \nThey need some too.
No, I'm almost empty right now. \nSidor is getting a shipment tomorrow. Or rather, tonight.
It's a bit too far to \nwalk over to his place.
Well, don't walk. \nAsk for delivery.
It's expensive. And there's no guarantee the delivery will make it. \nI heard the dogs have multiplied at the Garbage again?
Kolyan, I still won't be able to help you. \nSo - go to Sidor. Well, you can also ask Shinkar. :)
Yeah, very funny.
:)) So - go to Sidor. \nNo question about it.
Thanks, that's very comforting.
Igoresha, help me out!
What is it this time?
I need to lay low urgently. \nDisappear for about two weeks.
Well... what about me?
Look, someone might come to you or your guys \nand ask about me. \nThey know I hunted for loot with you. Tell them I went towards Agroprom...
Tell them I definitely went there... \nOr even better, add that someone from Agroprom was saying \nthe dogs chewed me up, well, something like that.
Bite your tongue! \nIs it really that bad?
It's bad :( \nAnd tell your guys there to keep their stories straight too - \nI went to Agroprom and haven't come back yet.
Got it. \nAnd where can I find you, just in case?
Sorry, I can't tell you yet. \nI'll find you myself. When things settle down...
Understood... Take care.
If everything turns out alright, \nI'll return the favor, I won't forget.
Don't worry about it. \nWe'll settle up later.
So what did they dig up there?
Where?
Where, where... in Karaganda. \nIn that underground lab you were talking about, remember?
Are you an idiot? \nI was just telling you what people are babbling about around here. \nWe weren't even planning to...
Just try getting in there. \nAnd if you do get in, you won't get out...
Then what was all the talk for? \nI heard some hotshot went down there...
They say he dragged out so much, \nthat he could buy up the whole Zone now.
Ha! Keep listening to them... \nSomeone's just driving up their own price. Cheap talk...
The way I heard it, \nnobody can even find the entrance, \nand you're telling me he 'dragged out...' Just talk.
And how do you know \nthat the entrance can't be found? That means someone was looking for it... \nAnd probably not just to find out, but because they wanted to go down.
Well then go find out, \nand go yourself, since you're so brave.
Come on... \nBut still, if you find anything out - let me know?
You bet!
Hey.
Yeah...
Did you hear about Kartavy? \nWhat do you make of it?
What's there to say, he went off the rails... \nHe flew into the anomaly himself.
You think he did it himself?

You bet! The guys were right there, they saw \nhim throwing bolts. They thought he wanted to walk around it, but he was doing the opposite - he was lining it up to be sure. \nAnd then he took a running leap right into it.
Well I'll be... Did he lose his mind?
He was already "cuckoo" even before that. \nHe screamed at night, and during the day kept harping on about some piece of the Monolith. He got all beaten down like that...
Do you remember, about a month ago, \nthere was another guy who kept talking about some monolithic pieces too?
Bobos or whatever his name was, Gundos?
Yeah, him. \nSo anyway, I saw him recently - you wouldn't recognize him. \nIt was almost the same with him - screaming at night, and during the day spouting some bullshit about the Monolith...
And then they found him strung up a few days ago. \nSomeone said that he pissed someone off real bad and the thugs strung him up...
Only I think he did it himself. \nHe was walking around like a ghost lately. \nWho could he have pissed off that much...
He went crazy too. There's something in common here.
A coincidence, probably.
But what is this piece of the Monolith? \nSome kind of artifact?
Oh, come on... \nI don't know and I don't want to know. "Jellyfish" are enough for me.
But still?
It's just nonsense. \nAre you going to listen to every nutjob?
Well it's not for nothing... \nAlright, forget it. If I find anything out myself, I'll tell you.
To hell with that! Don't even think about it! \nI don't even want to know - I'll sleep better.
When will the money be there? I'm waiting.
Hey! \nWe had a deal - on the thirtieth!
I need it right now.
Where am I supposed to get it right now? \nI don't have work until the day after tomorrow.
Do what you want, but I need the money the day after tomorrow. \nI helped you out, right? So now you help me out.
I just don't have it right now! \nAnd I have nothing to sell, you know that!
Do you have a rifle? \nGo see Sidor. He was offering to take out some asshole, pays well.
No, I'm not a merc. I won't go. \nWhy don't you go yourself?
I'm busy with other things right now! \nI have to pay back a ton of dough to the gang the day after tomorrow. I'm also going to work, I'll be back the morning after tomorrow...
I'll have part of the money, \nplus your debt. I don't give a damn, got it? \nFind it wherever you want, otherwise I'm turning your debt over to the gang.
Have you gone completely off the rails?
That's it, you've been warned.
Are you at Agro? \nWhat the hell is going on at Agroprom?!
There's all kinds of crap there. \nWhich exactly are you interested in? :)
The kind where the military \nreinforced the checkpoint at the passage from the Garbage. \nConstant duty, day and night...
And whoever I ask - \nnobody has a clue. You don't know anything? \nMaybe someone over there heard something about it?
I don't think so. \nWhat about making a deal? No way?
They won't even let you get close, almost shot me. \nFired warning shots right over my head.
Yeah... I just asked Sidor - \nhe's looking at me with wide eyes. He's going to find out now. \nIt really is some kind of crap...
Maybe they're running an operation? \nGo back there tomorrow, they're unlikely to keep the reinforcement up for long. \nIt's a hundred percent some one-off operation.
What tomorrow?! \nSanya Stepnoy fell into some well over there, \nbroke his leg, can't get out. He'll bleed to death in there!..
Ask Sidor, \nto at least let me in there alone!
Sidor says - not earlier than the day after tomorrow. \nTurns out, army brass has been hanging around there for the second day, got it?..
Better not even try, \nthey're guarded by elite special forces.
Animals. Looks like Sanya will die. \nIt's all so stupid...
What can I do? \nIt's the Zone, brother...
Hey. \nHave you been to the swamp in the Valley recently?
Well... what did you want?
In the Dark Valley near the swamps \nall the grass turned yellow overnight. In the morning we headed to the swamp \nfor artifacts, made it to the bridge, and turned right back...
We don't know if it's safe to go there or not. \nEverything around is green, but near the swamps - it's yellow. \nIt's so unnatural. Like it was painted.
Are there fleshes roaming around there?
Nobody. Like they all died off. \nThat's what's so scary. But a whole hotbed of artifacts appeared there overnight. \nThere are even "Stars" and "Fish" lying around, can you imagine?
Write to Semenov. \nHe knows about this kind of stuff.
I already wrote to him. He's silent. \nAnd we're itching to go, but it's too risky.
To hell with them, don't go. \nYou only get one life, as they say.
Yeah. But it's such a shame...
So what's up, where are you? \nDidn't forget about our talk?
Which one? Remind me. \nI was a bit out of it yesterday, might have forgotten...
Did you get hammered yesterday? \nWe were supposed to go on a trek. \nThat's not how you do business.
Well, I had a little... \nAnd I remember about the trek, just remind me where to and what for...
Get out of here! \nI'm not going with you, you've probably already had a hair of the dog by now. \nAnd I have to drag you? To hell with that...
And I dragged myself to the farm like an idiot, \nthinking you were already there... \nYou set me up.
Why are you offended? \nAlright, I'll settle up with you later, \nI have a few rounds in my stash...
Don't worry about it. \nI really can't go today anyway. \nWe'll link up some other time.
To hell with you, chatterbox. \nYou're only good at flapping your gums...
Heard a chopper went down?
I heard, so what?
They say it was bringing grub for the military. \nAll kinds of provisions. We should stock up for free. What do you say?
How do you know \nit was grub and for the military?
Vovan dragged over a whole crate of stew, \nbarely made it. Now he's walking around all proud. \nAnd the military is guarding the chopper. The crate just got thrown far away, and he snatched it.
Maybe it wasn't from the chopper, \nand was just lying around...
Yeah right... what just lies around in the Zone? \nDefinitely from the helicopter. And if there's one crate, there's more.
Like the military is just going to give it to you. \nThey're really waiting for you.
We'll make a deal, trade. \nWe can try... Maybe there's more unclaimed stuff lying around somewhere.
No, I'm not going. \nYou prove to them that we're there for business... \nThey'll just pop us like looters.
Suit yourself. Don't ask me later.
Hey, brother. I'm in trouble. :(
What is it this time?
I was in that place, I saw it. \nBut you can't reach it alone and it's crawling with snorks. \nBarely fought them off. One of them bit me. :(
Well then cauterize the bite with iodine \nor brilliant green and bandage it up.
I already did. \nOnly I'm afraid something might happen. \nI won't turn into a snork, will I?
You probably just impaled yourself on a branch out of fear, \nwhen you saw the snork :) It's all fairy tales - \nabout ghouls, about werewolves and the rest...
Do you think \nyou're going to grow a trunk now, or what?..
A dog bit me, \nand I didn't turn into a dog. Forget about it! You're alive and that's good. \nHeal up, then we'll go together.
A dog is one thing, but a snork is another. \nThey say their saliva is poisonous...
I won't grow a trunk, of course, \nbut what if I start degrading and become like them.
Oh, stop whining!
Look at Maloy, he was telling us \nhow a bloodsucker clipped him. \nSo he says his blood doesn't clot well now...
And it gets worse every day. \nHe's already afraid to cut sausage - \nwhat if he cuts himself and can't stop the bleeding.
Maloy made up that tall tale about the bloodsucker \nhimself. If it had "clipped" him, \nthe dogs would have finished eating Maloy a long time ago...
He's always been a liar and a coward, and still is.
Easy for you to say, you weren't bitten by a snork.
Tell the guys that as soon as you start growling, \nthey should shoot you, so you don't bite anyone else. :)
All you do is joke. \nAnd my whole arm is swollen. :(
Bah! You're annoying! \nI'm signing off, I'm sick of your whining.
How are things with you guys? What are you doing?
Nothing. Sitting around, waiting.
What are you waiting for? The weather?
Nah... waiting for some truck.
What?!
Well, we made a deal with the eggheads. \nThey threw some work our way: according to their calculations some truck is supposed to drive through here...
We have to spot it, film everything. \nThey gave us food for seven days for this...
How they calculate it, I don't know. \nThey just said that if there's no vehicle this week, then we can stop waiting.
Are you completely sick?! \nThey're definitely trying to catch a "death truck"!
What do we care... It's a freebie. \nOur job is to wait and take pictures, we're not planning to catch corpses...
And there will be payment for this too, \nif it works out. Figure this, we don't even have to do anything - just sit in one spot for a week and that's it. \nAnd we don't even have to look for food.
And what are you going to take pictures of, dust from the wheels?
Nah... the road hits a blockade here. \nIt'll either crash, or stop, or turn around. If it even shows up...
It's all good. \nWe can't eat that much food. We aren't doing anything and we aren't even drinking vodka, \nso there will be a fair amount left over, even if we don't film anything...
And if we do, they'll give us money too. \nIt's a profit either way.
And how do they know it will drive through there?
Ask them. \nMaybe because they've been seen everywhere else, but not here yet? \nI don't know, that's why they're eggheads, to calculate things.
I wouldn't have agreed... \nheard what they babble about the trucks?
You didn't agree to go to the Valley either, \nshaking all the time - "there's this there, there's that there"... \nthat's why you're broke all the time...
Smart people find freebie work \nand don't shake like you do.
I'm not shaking. \nBut I still wouldn't wait for a truck.
Don't sweat it - all those tall tales about them are bullshit... \nAlright then - I'll tell you later.
Deal.
Hey, buddy. \nSo how are things with you?
Still just as shitty :(( \nWaiting for some doctor. The guys passed on that he's coming.
A real doctor?
How should I know, \nI can't afford to be picky right now.
Yeah... At least the place is secure, \nwhere you laid low?
It's alright. \nThe guys left grub and water, I'll last a couple of days... probably... \nGood thing there's a connection at least. They said they'd come back later.
Why didn't they just drag you out right away?
They didn't dare... \nSay they're afraid I'll fall to pieces, \nif they move me... heh-heh.
You're still joking? \nWell, that's just great then... Hang in there.
What else can I do... \nTruth be told, I'm remembering my life right now.
Well?
It's all shitty... \nand there's nothing to fix now, damn it... :(
Hey, stop that! \nEverything will be fine, the doctor will come and we'll pull you out.
Come on, who are you trying to fool... \nYou just, well... write to her, you know who...
Enough! Just hang in there! \nAt least for these couple of days, I'm asking you... \nHe'll definitely come. I'll find him myself, I'll drag him to you by the scruff of his neck!
I'll try...
Hey :)
Who is this? What do you want?
It's Kolyan. Remember Agrik?
Oh! Hey, buddy, sorry, didn't recognize you. \nI thought you had a different handle... \nThanks again for the meds, you really helped me out.
It's nothing :) \nListen, do you know Lyokha Cat?
Umm... is that the guy who was lying \nabout taking down a boar with a bayonet knife on a bet? \nWell, we sat by the fire a couple of times, why?
Yeah, him. I'm looking for him. \nIf you see him, tell him that Kolyan Money smashed his PDA against the wall while drunk, \nreplaced it and, well, you know... is restoring his address book...
Tell him not to knock on my line for now, \nif he's looking... And the handle is definitely different now, \nI decided to change it with the new PDA, because people were already getting on my nerves.
I'll pass it on. Well... and how are you yourself?
I can't reach him. \nThanks, take care.
So have you decided? \nAre you coming with us or what?
Exactly how long is the trek there? \nAnd what's the situation like in general?
Well, who's going to tell you the truth, pal? \nHowever our luck falls, that's how it'll be...
But, like Mokryak said: \na week of fear - and your whole mug is in marmalade. \nWell, are you signing up?
If everyone is going, then I am too, clearly! \nWhen are we moving out?
We're stocking up tomorrow. \nYou get some gear too, nobody is going to carry sausage and ammo for you. \nAnd be there sharp the day after tomorrow, don't be late!
I'll be there.
Tyulen, by God, you're a sap. \nWhat did you do?
What kind of nonsense is this?
You were singing like a nightingale by the fire yesterday, \nthat there's so much loot lying at the Farm - you couldn't haul it out with a dump truck!..
Do you know how many people will rush there now? \nIt's probably already packed tight in there...
Only the dogs and cats are bolting out of there, \nwith their eyes bulging - they've never seen so many stalkers \nin one place!..
Why did you blab? \nNow you won't find anything there except trampled grass!
You better watch your mouth, actually...
Anyway, Tyulen, that's not how things are done. \nWhat's ours is ours, no need to flap your gums...
Are you going to give me orders? \nDo I owe you or something? About yesterday...
Did you clear the path with me at the Farm? \nOr, maybe, you covered my back while I was looking for a way? Did you share your last with me?
If good people asked me \nto keep an eye on you, then I kept an eye on you. \nNow I see who, or rather, what you are...
And that's it... \nThen I don't know you anymore either.
Do you know \nwhere I can get my suit fixed?
What do you mean where? \nHead to the Bar, what - didn't you know?
I know. But to hell with that. \nThose red-and-black guys declared my persona non grata. \nGot a message from a Duty member today. They tracked me down...
Holy shit... \nWhat happened? How did it happen?
I stepped into an anomaly, miraculously tore myself out of it. \nIt's all in shreds, just rags hanging...
I was dazed myself, fell down nearby and I hear \nthe bushes rustling, so I just let loose a double-barrel blast right in there.
Dead?
I don't know. \nThey hit the dirt immediately and tossed a couple of grenades over. \nOnce I realized I'd shot at Duty members, I just booked it right from my hands and knees.
The anomaly saved me, took the shrapnel upwards, \nand I just hauled ass on all fours to the Garbage. \nI'm lying here now, hurting. Turns out, I broke my left arm, figure that.
Oh, you motherf... \nAlright, wait, I'll be right back...
So, here's the deal. \nI had a chat with a guy here. Goes by Prokhor. \nHe said one guy got his arm pierced, Lepyokha Sery...
But, he says, he saw him - nothing serious was hit there. \nHe's on his feet and walking patrol already, near the base, true...
Well, they're generally reasonable guys, \nI told them about you, we discussed the issue. \nIn short - once you recover a bit, come over...
I have about three liters, \nyou owe an artifact for each of them - two pieces, that is, get them ready. \nAnd they have no further complaints.
Brother, thank you! \nI owe you, by God!
Come on, we'll settle up :))
You know what I saw yesterday? \nSome kind of new anomaly. \nThese white sparks flying up and down...
I threw a little rock in there, \nand it hung there for a couple of minutes at first, \nthen started spinning, and whoosh! - it's gone.

With your experiments 
you're going to catch a rock like that to the head someday, and your climbing days will be over. 
Go to the eggheads, ask to be a lab assistant, you'll be advancing science, 
making humanity happy :))
Wash test tubes for them? \nNo, thanks, I'll manage somehow myself, \nand I need to take care of myself first anyway...
I was just interested - \nsuch an unseen thing, no idea why or where from. \nRemember when I tried to photograph a blowout back then?
Ah... when you set your \npoint-and-shoot on a timer, I remember. \nBut you said your point-and-shoot died after that.
I thought so too, dumped it in my stash. \nBut then I started sorting through my junk, picked it up and turned it on.
Well?
The first few frames are dark, \nthen overexposure - solid white haze. \nBut the last two - a face.
Whose?
A woman's, I think. Hard to tell. \nI aimed the camera at the sky, anyway, when I see you - I'll show you.
Want to hear a joke?
What? Go ahead...
At the Cordon, Sidorovich \nordered a "Stone Blood" from some green rookie...
And the guy asks him: \n"But do stones really have blood?"
Where am I supposed to laugh?
Just wait... \nSidrych says to him: \n"This, son, is the Zone. Anything is possible here"...
And this blockhead went past the road \nand started shooting at rocks with all his might, trying to extract blood :))
Wasted a ton of ammo, \nand never got any blood from a stone. Good thing a military patrol \nspotted it in time and quickly booked it somewhere towards the ATP.
:) Quite the actor... The idiots out there...
Hey, are you busy?
No, we're sitting by the fire right now, \nmanaged to get some tea. Man it's good, haven't had any in a long time. \nIt's always vodka, vodka, getting sick of it already. :) What did you want?
I don't even know how to say it... \nHow are you doing yourself after the last blowout?
Seems fine... \nand what's wrong with you? Did it clip you?
It doesn't seem like it, but I'm sketching out all the time. \nIt's terrifying. Nightmares, running thoughts about God knows what...
Here's a piece of crap, for example: \nthe generator at our base died. \nWhat with this and that, it's already evening. But the light bulbs are glowing!..

You couldn't tell during the day, but once it got dark - \nwell hello there - they're glowing. \nThe shadows from these lamps are kind of blue and it's like they're moving on their own, I swear to you!..

I'm standing there, looking at my shadow, \nand it's waving its hand at me. I'm so scared I'm even afraid to go take a leak alone, \nI keep rubbing shoulders with the guys, and they're giving me sideways glances... What is this crap?
No antirads, of course? \nFixing yourself up with vodka? \nWell, looks like you've fixed yourself up enough...
What do you mean?
I mean you need to drink less! \nSwitch to tea, like we did here. That's the whole problem.
DTs, you think? Bah! \nAnd I was... I even got scared. Well thanks, you calmed me down...
The other day in the Gut I stumbled \nupon a dead military stalker raiding party. \nThey were looted thoroughly before me, and the dogs had already snacked on them...
And the devil made me go down into a ravine \nnearby. Found a huge metal suitcase there, \ncovered all over in seals...
Couldn't crack it open on the spot. \nSo figure this, I dragged it off to my stash at the Cordon. \nI cursed up a storm while I was carrying it: it must be about 25 kilos in there!..
Now I'm sitting here and thinking: \nwhat for? \nWhat am I supposed to do with this suitcase now?
Give it to Sidorovich.
How did I not figure that out myself?! \nHe pays very well for trinkets like that. \nThanks, Captain Obvious! :)
So how much longer are we going to wait for you sitting on our asses? \nWe're going to leave without you.
Hell if I know. \nMaybe it's not worth waiting. It's the third day Kuznetsov isn't at the post under the bridge! \nAnd his replacement got whacked...
Now a legal crossing of the embankment isn't happening! \nI beat Stas, twice. \nI wanted to trade my whole stash of vodka for ammo from Kuznetsov. Now I'll have to get drunk.
Well then don't miss your mouth! \nSee ya.
And good luck to you. \nThere's barely a handful of ammo anyway. \nWhere am I going to go on a raid with that kind of ammunition. Some other time.
Arkasha, have you tried using a flamethrower? \nIs there any point in renting one?
Haven't tried a flamethrower - it's too heavy. \nAnd in my stalker suit, every gram counts.
Ok! Got it. \nGuess there's no point in me messing with it either.
Pakhom... son of a bitch! \nI brought him a jar of "Slime", dumped it on the counter, \nand he just grunted and doesn't even want to talk.
Pakhom doesn't like repeating himself. \nAnd he doesn't like it when people don't listen to him. \nHe probably told you about a COUPLE of jars, and you brought one.
I thought it was just a figure of speech... \nBut Pakhom is a man of principle, it seems!
Tell me, how do the scientists at Yantar \nbehave in terms of stealing?..
I'm thinking of making a stash right in the bunker. \nI hope science hasn't gotten too hungry in the Zone.
They don't seem to steal. \nPersonally, I kept weapons, armor, and other junk there. And everything is fine.
Duty members steal and re-hide it \nsomewhere nearby - just like kids. \nLike evil kids - they mine it, the bastards!..
And then you just find it, \nbut you can't just take it :((
But the scientists there are alright, \nthey don't even suspect that someone is hiding something in their place. \nWell, as long as it isn't vodka.
To go back again \nthrough the exact same tunnel with "Electras"? But how?
Listen here, son. \nYou get a bolt out and wait for the last "Electra" in front of you to fade...
You run forward and stop \nwith enough distance before an active "Electra", \nso there is room for the next one. You watch when the "Electra" behind the active one goes out...
At this moment you throw a bolt \ninto the active "Electra", discharging it, and run like hell through it. \nDo the same with the second series of "Electras" - and you are at Sidorovich's.
Holy shit... \nI'll never get through there in my life.
Chicken? \nAnd you aren't shy about broadcasting it on the open channel...
Yeah, I want to live, \nI'm not embarrassed to talk about it and I don't plan to start being embarrassed.
Well, go on then... \nHumiliate yourself for a can of meat...
Well, I will. \nAnd not for canned meat... For sausage...
About cooking arts. \nSome flash when they fall out, \nsome don't flash. We pick it up and do it again...

Usually on the second or third round \nafter laying out the second (according to the recipe) ingredient (for example, for SF it's a bandage) \nthere is a long receiving flash, and the anomaly immediately pulls in the rest of the ingredients.
SF? \nFlash?
SF - stone flower. \nBlymayut (Ukr.) - means to flicker, to flash.
Ah, I see. \nAnd how to choose the anomaly? \nThey say you can't cook in just any of them.

An anomaly (any) for cooking 
must be chosen so that: 
1. the ingredients do not scatter, 
2. the result of the cooking can be easily picked up...
I.e. if we cook in a "Carousel", \nyou need to stand a little lower, \notherwise the cooked art can fly into space.
I just can't get past \nthe northern checkpoint! As soon as I poke my nose in, they're right there. \nI have to hightail it back.
Who are they? \nBandit marauders?
I wish! \nAt least you can negotiate with those. Tushkanos...
Tushkanos? You're kidding, pal... \nHaven't you tried shooting them?
I tried. \nBut they're too damn hard to kill. \nAnd I'm still a greenhorn - can't hit a damn thing.
Well then listen, "greenhorn", \ntheir nest is there. That's what they are protecting. \nSo grit your teeth and run until you pass the nest...
The tushkanos will leave you alone themselves. \nSure, they might bite your tender buttocks a couple of times. \nBut that's nothing. Later you can show the scars off to the girls - brag a little.
Thanks for the advice, uncle. \nI'll go test out your recommendation right now.
Go ahead. And give a ring when you get through. \nPeople are already betting vodka on you here...
I've heard many times that a stash \ncan be booby-trapped, but how exactly do you do it?..
Just put grenades in it? \nBut those are exactly what got stolen from me...
Maybe I shouldn't have come to the Zone... \nThis isn't for me...
What the hell? \nLyokha Tambovsky is wandering around Agroprom. \nI walk out to the Garbage, and who do you think it is?..
Lyokha Tambovsky, \nbut his mug and outfit are different. The Zone's tricks again?
No. Just two Lyokhas. \nAnd both are from Tambov.
Ara, listen: \ngot forty bottles with hard work. \nCarrying 20 from the Bar, Stas is guarding another 20 at Sidorovich's...
Came to the Cordon - no Sidorovich... business trip. \nSleep for a day - no Sidorovich. Sleep two - no Sidorovich...
Sleep three - still no! \nReturned on the fourth morning, the tourist! \nHere's the swag, give me the vodka. Run to the embankment - Kovalchuk...
Sleep a day - Kovalchuk, sleep two - Kovalchuk. \nOn the third - hooray! Kuzya returned...
Here, Kuzya, take your vodka, give me the AK-47. \nWow, good! Only slept for five days in total.
I've got a stalemate situation here... \nGrabbed vodka from Stas - and went to Kuznetsov to trade. But he's not there...
Some young guy is standing there instead of him. \nWent and slept for three days. Same guy again. \nSlept for three days again - same Kovalchuk...
What do I do? \nWhere do I sell the vodka?
Well, while you're sleeping, Kuzya is on duty.
Lyokha! Guide is a badass, it turns out! \nGet this, he cleared out a pack of dogs past the bridge! All by himself!!! Î_î
Bullshit! There were three of them there - \nGuide and two other stalkers. \nBut it's still badass...
I think that guy was there too, \nyou know, the geared up one... What's his name... Well, you know.
Nah.
He dropped by our village recently \nwith a "Vintorez", said hi at the campfire, and took off quickly.
And who is Vintorez?
You're such a boy scout! \nIt's this awesome sniper rifle...
Costs a fortune though, \nonce I earn enough, I'll buy myself one. \nListen, move to our village, why are you sitting there at the farm?
Nah, you've got military patrolling there. \nThey don't poke their noses in here.
What do we care about the military? \nSidorovich runs things here - everything is fine.
No, I'm good here too.
Your loss.
Kolyan, \ndo you know anyone from "Freedom"?
I do, but keep it between us. \nWhat did you want?
I need to contact Screw. \nNeed small screwdrivers, Phillips and Torx. \nReally small ones, like for watches.
Is it really necessary to go to the Army Warehouses \nfor them? Nobody closer has them?
Seems not, I asked around, \nalready pestered everyone here with these screwdrivers. And why go? \nSome of the stalkers go there one way or another anyway. Can just pass it along with them.
Do you have any idea how much they'll charge you for that? \nAnyway, if you really need it - I'll tell a guy, he'll write to you, say he's from me.
Yeah, thanks!
Vitek, we're burning, save us! \nBorov's guys have us surrounded in the Valley, near the factory. \nWe're not going to fight back, there's too many of them, we're giving up...
Please find two "Stars", \nand bring them here. Be near the bulldozer next to the swamp tomorrow morning...
Give the artifacts to their courier, \nand I'll pay you back later.
No need to look. \nKhimik has two "Stars". \nI'll borrow them. But mind you - it goes on your tab.
Whatever you want. \nJust keep it quiet, don't tell our guys. \nWe dug up something here, no need to make noise. ;)
I didn't get any of that, but I smell profit. :) \nI'll do it. Just be careful over there, don't act too tough, or they might just shoot you. \nAnd two "Stars" will be wasted. :))
Thx. That's it, don't write to me anymore.
Sanyok, hey! Heard the news? \nTurns out Sakharov is growing watermelons on the bunker grounds!
What kind of bullshit is that? \nHe's not growing any watermelons, I was just there yesterday.

But did you go behind the bunker? Exactly. \nHe's got a melon patch there. Write to him, make a deal, buy a watermelon from him, \notherwise you'll be waiting forever for something tasty from outside the perimeter.
I kinda doubt it... \nI can't write to him, but if I'm there anytime soon - I'll find out...
Why did you even decide \nthat he has a melon patch there? Ah... got it, like a joke?
Not a joke. \nSomeone told me today. \nAnd some Marked One apparently told them.
Found the right guy to listen to... \nThey say he's not quite right in the head. But fine, I'll find out.
Where could I get my hands on more \ngrenades for the underbarrel, seeing as I haven't been coming across many of them yet...
And also... \nis it worth getting a shortened Dragunov sniper rifle from Sultan? \nIs it a good tool, anyone used it?
For which underbarrel? \nIf for ours, then do some work for Voronin - he'll reward you...
If for the NATO one - \nyou can trade Sidorovich for a mutate.
Seryoga, there's an order from the eggheads. \nThree snorks, alive...
Deliver to Yantar on Sunday. \nAre we taking it?
What are they paying?
Artifacts, what else? \nWhen did they ever pay with anything else?..
I don't know which ones exactly yet, \nthe main thing is to decide in principle - do we take it or not.
No, I pass, I have things to do. \nGive Kozmin a whistle, maybe he'll want to?
And generally, let the stalkers catch them. \nThere's too much hassle with live ones.
Suit yourself.
Hi!
Same to you! :)
Listen, \ncan we read the eggheads' PDAs?
No. We can, but it's very labor-intensive. \nNot cost-effective.
The hell it's not cost-effective! \nIt's a gold mine. Any expenses will pay off!
Not necessarily. \nThey might pay off, but only if you know \nexactly whose to read and that will cost money too.
So what? Little by little...
In the Old Man's squad they started hacking them once. \nThey also thought they'd find out all the secrets, but it was just chatter about the properties of an "Electra"...
Spent a ton of money, and found out \nwhat they already knew. Do you have something specific?
Just asking, out of curiosity mainly... \nThere is something, of course.
Know too much - \nwon't live to get old... :)
If you have something \nvery specific and profitable, we can try to push for resources from Papa. \nBut it's unlikely.
How would I know what's going to be there? \nI don't have anything yet, just some ideas that we could score something...
Don't advertise it too much. \nI bet you are taking it without asking :)).
I'm not that stupid... \nAlright, later.
Good morning! Want to have a laugh?
You bet!
Remember how Oleg bet Arnie yesterday \nthat he could make it to Yantar and back in a day?
Yeah, he is supposed to be back \nby noon today. Why?
He won't be back. :)
What's funny about that? Did he die?
Nope, alive and well, \nhe's on the road from the Wild Territory to the Bar right now. \nExcept he won't make it to Arnie.
Why? \nGot hammered on vodka at the checkpoint again?
Two "Whirligigs" formed \nright in the passageway here. Right next to each other. No way to go around...
Only if he rushes through the Army Warehouses. \nOr waits until the next blowout.
So he is standing there and swearing. \nAll our guys gathered on this side and are laughing their asses off. \nBasically, Oleg is buying the drinks. :)))
Well, that's not fair. \nIf it wasn't for the anomalies, he would have won.
Why is it not fair? \nThe deal was - from the Bar to Yantar and back to the Bar in twenty-four hours...
And nobody said anything about anomalies. \nThis is the Zone, pal :)))
Yeah, that's a joke. Unlucky. :)
Tell me, please, what kind of kindergarten is this? \nSome kind of ideology...
These factions. Why do you need all this? \nYou will lose yourself as a scientist by engaging in this nonsense...
I even have a suspicion \nthat this is all the result of some kind of radiation, \ncompletely blocking the critical perception of the world...
Excuse me, \nbut these social associations of yours based on ideological principles are simply ridiculous.
My dear professor, \nthat is not quite true. First and foremost, we set ourselves the goal \nof protecting the world from the influence and emanations of the Zone.

But colleague, 
allow me to still call you that for now. 
These goals have long been successfully fulfilled by military organizations. 
Why meddle in an area that is completely outside your function?
The military is doing a decent job with this task, \nbut they perform purely protective functions...
But they do not always manage to stop the spread \nof those notorious artifacts. \nIn fact, they mostly fail. And that is no secret.
I will not argue with you here. \nThe facts, as they say, speak for themselves. \nBut why do you need this?
Professor, \nwe have debated this issue with you multiple times...

The influence of artifacts on the human body \nis practically unstudied, and yet they are already being dragged past the perimeter in full force, \nand used there with what they think are their identified properties...
They turned this into a profitable business, \nbut they don't want to think about how and what this will result in...
So someone has to, \nas pretentious as it may sound, stand guard over the interests of humanity.
God, how ridiculous it is to just drop everything \nand join some gang that covers up its dealings \nwith high ideals and loud slogans...
And you bought into these slogans. \nAfter all, you would be of much greater use doing science...
Well, we have started the conversation from the beginning... \nExcuse me, I have to go.
Max, you are a fool.
What makes you say that? Didn't get your morning drink or something?
Do you even realize what you're doing, damn it? \nHow can you drag this filth out of the Zone, try to understand that, you smoke-filled head!..
What if some "Slime" \n(ugh, disgusting, came up with a name too) \ngoes off somewhere in Kyiv tomorrow and blows away half the city?..
Can you guarantee that this won't happen?
Dude, you are wrong. Want me to prove it?
Look, I have a friend, \nout there, beyond the perimeter. You know, he named his kid after me. \nAnd why? Because his wife was diagnosed with cancer a year ago.
And they sent her home, \ngiving her a permanent disability group. That's how it's done \nwhen there are no chances left. He wrote to me about it via email.
I had to, of course, \npush myself seriously, and I pushed the guys too, but it was worth it. \nNow I know for sure that I'm not hanging around here for nothing, because I helped a friend.
What options did he have \nwithout your "filth from the Zone"? Tell me, dude...
They say you found something special, \nthat has Sakharov dancing a jig in the bunker for three days now? Come on, spill it! :)
Not me. Our guys found it. \nA little notebook somewhere at Yantar. Torn like an old sock! \nAnd you can't even make out what's written there...
But I made out some of it \nand copied it down to my PDA. Look:
"...Prevent the possibility \nof any unauthorized personnel entering the Object's area of effect... \nWe need to localize the possible consequences of its operation"...
The personnel has practically no control over the situation. \nThe Object is affecting the environment, and the results are already taking place. \nThe Object has localized in the local complex..."
What the hell is this?
You're the hell! \nThe guys used the notebook for kindling, \nbut Sakharov promised me a new generation device for these notes.
You can see all the anomalies plain as day. \nI'll be walking like it's a park! \nI purposefully haven't shown him everything yet, told him there's a lot...
And whatever is missing, I'll write in myself :) \nHere, take a look at what else was there:

"...Personnel are ordered to take all measures. 
It is also necessary to prevent access to the Object and, if possible, localize all cases of local flora and fauna 
falling under radiation at the site of the breach. 
The use of technical means must be proportionate to the degree of danger..."
Cool... Is there more?
You bet!
"...I do not understand why there is no connection with the center? \nWe have been here for three weeks already, like on a desert island..."
"...like they broke off their chains. \nHowever, it has been clear for a long time that this is a risky experiment. \nGod knows what is going on..."
You probably made up \nall this gibberish yourself, and Sakharov fell for it :))
You should ask him for a ratchet \nagainst mutants, have him gift it to you. \nYou'll spin it, make some noise, and they'll all run away...
Don't believe it - don't, \nwhy did you ask then...
"Freedom" are all a bunch of stoned idiots. \nDid you hear what they pulled off again?
Are you talking about the dog?

Yeah. And the main thing is, 
they deliberately put on a show, here you go, Duty members, suck on this: 
we'll transport a live pseudodog and we won't ask you! 
And past the perimeter, too! And with an armed escort! Might as well have a marching band...
How did San Sanych allow such a thing?
How... just like that. Don't you know? \nThey got bent over by the headquarters...
Some slicker arrived to exchange experience. \nA present for the foreign colleagues...

Sanych is pissed, yelling at his guys... \nIt's even for the best that the Freedom guys caught the beast, because if the soldiers had taken casualties, \nSanych would have torn that slicker's throat out, you know him.
I know. That's the only plus. \nIf San Sanych gets removed, such a mess will start here, God forbid...
Seventh to Third. Come in! \nWhat's going on over there? Why aren't you answering on the radio?
Yeah, well, we pushed our luck! \nGot almost right up to the second structure, \nand there's some crap flying around. Transparent and silent...
Slavik got covered in bricks right away. \nAlong with the radio. Looks like that piece of shit is throwing them.
Then run from there! \nThe fifth squad is already on their way to you.
No way. The exit is caved in, we're blocked... \nWe fired a volley from the underbarrels - part of the wall collapsed right in the passage, and there's no way around.
It's fine. Not the first time. \nWe'll dig you out. Just wait for it.
Unlikely this time... \nGenka and Sanyok are done for, and my leg is broken... I'm on Promedol for now...
That piece of shit is dragging itself around somewhere nearby... \nThat's it, I'm screwed... The zombies are coming...
Third... come in...
Third...
Oh for fuck's sake! \nThird...
Antokha, did you see about the gifts for our general? \nWhat do you say?..
Let's give him a flask, with an engraving. \nBut what should we engrave?
Heh! Let's do a wolf's profile and the inscription: \n"Greetings from the Army Warehouses" :)))
:))) Only then you'll be the one handing it over - \nhe won't shoot you. :))) But seriously?
Oh, stop messing around. \nThe guys already prepared one for him, an SVU with inlay. Beautiful. \nLeshchov made it, they got the gold from Yantar.
Oh, well alright then. What do we have for tomorrow? \nBecause we wanted to rush out for "mushrooms" in the morning.
Seems like nothing. \nGo ahead, just go early and not for too long, you never know.
Roger that.
Roger my ass. Got it.
Just spend two weeks getting treated on the mainland, \nand you can't recognize the base anymore. What's up with the Arena?
It kicked the bucket. \nOr rather, the general ordered it. Said it's time to stop running sweepstakes here.
Oh wow! And what about Arnie?
Arnie has become a moneychanger now. \nYou can trade in ammo of a caliber you don't need and take what you do...
And further down Yurko Lisaped set up shop. \nFixes weapons. Quite high quality, I'll tell you. And not expensive.
Got it. Good to know.
Hey. \nI found something out.
What?
Did you forget? \nRemember that place where you can't get through the "Slimes"?..
Well, a stalker here was bragging to me \nthat he knows a way to walk through "Slime" without problems. \nSays he was provided with the intel at Yantar.
Well, and?
He's asking twenty thousand for the info.
Look at him go, huh! \nAlright, I'll contact the eggheads right now. Stall for time...
And notify Petrenko, \nwe'll have to publicly execute this scumbag if it's not true. \nWhich, by the way, I have no doubt of.
No problem, \nI said I'll gather the money tomorrow, and you find out in the meantime...
Stall him, \njust don't let this creep out of your sight, so he doesn't leave. \nHe might be sent by "Freedom", you never know.
Alright.
Has anyone among the esteemed gentlemen \ncooked these "Electro Beads" yet?..
The recipe I got says: \nneeds an EH liver. But Semyonov definitely mentioned a kidney. \nHow am I supposed to understand this?..
I tried cooking in three different "Snowballs", \nand all without result. True, my "Mama's Beads" have already "weathered"...
Maybe I need new ones, \nor is it all about the right anomaly? Please explain.
Well, you definitely got scammed... \nEither by the guy who gave you the recipe, or Semyonov, or the "Mama's Beads", or the "Snowball" failed you... \nOr maybe all of them together.
You just want to grin and laugh. \nAnd I have a tragedy here, you understand...
Then why don't you publish the recipe. \nMaybe the whole world will pitch in and help...
Yeah, right! \nKiss my ass, gentlemen! I'll figure it out myself.
And he started off so well: "esteemed gentlemen..."
Hey. \nWhat do you think about that last shootout?
I think \nsomeone leaked the information in advance. Do you have doubts too? \nThey couldn't have just ended up there like that. Someone definitely sold us out.
That's true, but who should we suspect. \nDoesn't seem like you or me. And not Vasilich...
So who is it - \nEars or that guy all in the new outfit? \nI didn't like him from the start.
That's not a given yet. \nOur own guys could have set us up too...
Remember last week \nwe got into a drunken argument with the second squad?
Are you crazy? \nSure, all kinds of trash talk happens when you're drunk, but to set up your own guys like this afterwards...
You're exaggerating... \nI'm telling you for sure it's that guy, what's his name, got a funny street name too. \nWe need to press him a little and feel him out...
Well alright, let's roll up on him this evening. \nYou, me, and we'll take Vasilich - almost everyone who was there, and we'll have a frank conversation.
Let's do it, or he might slip away... Come over.
Where is that damn Petrenko? \nIvantsov has been harping on for a week that he went to Sakharov to scalp artifacts...
Well he's not there, \nI wasn't too lazy to check.
They say he's catching a female snork at Yantar \nto fulfill his sickest desires...
Or he's getting the consequences of those desires \ntreated at the Swamp Doctor's...
It's funny to you, but I need him for official business.
I'll snip off the wants and desires \nof all you wiseguys right now, without the Swamp Doctor...
And you stay right there. \nI'll be there in an hour.
By the way, I'll ask then: \ndoes the moneychanger at the Bar also take paper recipes and some other items, in exchange for ammo?..
I got more than 20 packs \n(~600 rounds) of expensive ammo for one recipe. Is this a scam?
He sells them for that - let him, is that a bad thing? \nMaybe he's really interested in recipes.
Fedyay, did you see the message from the eggheads?
What kind of nonsense is that?
Well, a message. \nIn the "inbox" folder, look in the general channel.
Ah! I can't figure out \nthis devil-machine at all. I barely found the messages from you...
Where to look in this calculator? \nWell, what's it about?
They are looking for their smart guy, \nwho disappeared somewhere near Yantar. \nReward and honor to the finders, as expected.
And what kind of dude is he?
Some Vasiliev guy. \nHaven't heard anything?
Some guy passed by,\n all crazed and ragged. And his suit was that... red one. \nWell, the ones they always wander around in...
It was the day before yesterday, \nafter two in the afternoon. Didn't approach us, stomped right past... \nor rather, he was walking on autopilot...
And a flask in his hand - he was mumbling something. \nI reported it to the superior, said, seems like the zombified are coming...
He chewed me out for it too: \n"Said, seems like, and kind of - what kind of report is this from a spotter". \nI don't know anything else... And that guy just walked off towards the bunker.
Nah. Timing-wise, doesn't seem like him...
Why did you come at that stalker yesterday? \nCouldn't share something?
Me? Came at him? Seriously?
Don't you remember how you were yelling: \n"I'd rather see your Marked One in a coffin!" And you smashed that stalker in the mug...
What does he have to do with it? \nYou should have smashed that Marked One's mug... \nAnd I got caught in the crossfire.
Yeah... Well, alright, my bad, I admit it. \nWhat's it to you? Why are you sulking?
Why? You kicked me in the eye with your boot \nand it's like, sorry, I didn't mean it? It's a dick move to do that...
What, really? \nMan, I don't remember anything, honest. Drank like a fish...
And then again - \nI've got a shiner under my own eye, that's not for nothing...
Yeah, hell of a party...
Hey, what's the news over there?
Same old. And you?
Sancha passed on a message here. \nHe's in the district hospital right now, chilling in surgery...
Got in there easy, \nslipped the girls in the waiting room a couple of ten-spots, \nand now they're waiting on him hand and foot...
And he told the doc he got ventilated on a hunting trip. :) \nWell, that guy's understanding... By the way, he's hinting \nthat he wouldn't mind going "hunting" himself.
That's an idea. \nWe need to drag this doc over here, along with the nurses :))
Sancha said he'd work on him, \nand, don't sweat it, he'll show up at our place :)
Good. Because the Swamp Doctor has gotten completely out of hand. \nOnly patches up stalkers, and doesn't even show his nose here...
Hey! Did you hear what our potheads pulled off?
Nah. What are you talking about?
Ah! You haven't heard yet!? \nWell sit back, read this :))
Max's crew recently started walking around \nwith these mysterious mugs \n"Oh, what we know, and we won't tell you"...
Whispering, scheming something \nand then the whole crowd bailed somewhere. Max himself didn't understand where they disappeared to. \nThen - bam!
They showed up a day later. \nBuck naked. Absolutely naked, in their birthday suits and that's it!
No way! You're shitting me...
Man, everyone at the base was there. \nAnd the laughter was non-stop, till we turned blue. \nLukash thought they got mugged by thugs, but they just mumble, keep quiet and that's it...
The boys and I interrogate them: \nspill it, like, maybe you got so severely high, \nand the trip was so bad that you came galloping back naked?
So did you find out? Don't keep me in suspense..
Max cracked them after all. \nThey found some moss nearby. Purple kind of and it sparkles...
These experimenters tried everything, \nso they smoked this moss...
And what, does it really hit that hard?
That's an understatement, a total trip! \nBut they didn't show up naked from the high, the moss actually ate everything on them, imagine that?
They plucked it in the woods, \nsmoked it right there, and dropped, like they died. \nAnd the moss, it ate their suits, and assault rifles, everything, except them.
Is it like in a "Greed"?
No... it's somehow different. \nMax says: when they came to, they thought they were hallucinating...
Except it wasn't a hallucination, \nand they actually had to shake their balls all the way back to base. \nGood thing nobody ate them on the way...
Ah! So that's where the naked guy came from! \nRemember, he showed up at our place? \nExcept he, apparently, got so high that he's still sitting and mumbling...
Now there's going to be a pilgrimage to that clearing, \nI'm telling you for sure. Chu River Valley :)) \nLukash is already scratching his head on how to keep people out...
Like you can hold them back :) \nEveryone will have an itch up their ass now until they try it. :)
Read this message and pass it on!
Dudes, free stalkers! \nThere's a specific topic.
Take a "Jellyfish", \nscrape it carefully with a knife. \nYou need about half a matchbox full.
Then keep this matchbox \nnear a "Slime" for a week. \nMix what you get with tobacco.
And that's it - puff away to your health! :) \nA clear high is guaranteed!
A direct exit to space and to Jah! \nSend your thanks in vodka. :)
They say the Duty boys \ngot caught in the Dark Valley. Is that right?
Yeah, seems so... \nThey were sniffing around there, and the boys nabbed them.
And what about them? Will they be ransomed?
Who needs these morons? \nTheir boss probably doesn't give a damn - one more, one less... \nThey'll recruit new meat, there are enough fools.
A buddy of mine whispered to me: \nthey are keeping them in a cage there. \nThey are psychos, you know yourself, they immediately start trying to kill everyone.
Yeah, "kill 'em!" - they love that :)) \nAnd then they put a gas mask on their head and jump around :)
Spot on! :)
Heard Sanka Cheerful got his leg blown off?
Where? Is he alive himself?
Alive, but in a very bad way. \nZombies swarmed the Barrier, he got hit by shrapnel, shattered his leg completely...

The guys shot him up with painkillers, bandaged him, \nand surrounded him with some artifacts. He's holding on, but between you and me, \nit would have been better if he died outright. Where is he going to go without a leg now?
Feel sorry for the guy. \nI'll reach out to a lad, he seems to know someone who worked as a doctor...
Or a paramedic, I can't say for sure. \nI'll go to him, have a chat about Cheerful, the chance is small, but what if.
Go, of course. \nArtifacts are artifacts, but real medical help will be more reliable. \nMaybe something will work out.
Hey, man! \nI know you're receiving this. \nDo you think there are only stoned junkies here and they didn't notice you?..
We've seen all sorts here. \nAnd you're sniffing around to spy and report back? \nThat's your duty, yeah?..
So I'll tell you myself \nand pass it to your boss: if not for us, you'd all be dead right away! \nWho protects the Barrier, huh?
Just morons!
But they are our morons. \nWhile your snot-chewers shoot bandits with little pistols, and ten of them pop a couple of dogs and immediately yell: \n"We are the defenders against the Zone!"
Oh, go to hell, you whining junkie!
You holy shit, \nstop barking and don't rub around the base, \nbut take a walk to the Barrier and see what kind of little animals are lying around there...
You haven't even seen the likes of them. \nGo, man, and don't shine your triple-magnification binoculars, you are very visible through the scope...
Oh, and the suit Voronin issued you \nis lousy. Even our rookies don't walk around in such padded jackets...
Bye, ugly! \nAnd change your diaper - it stinks all the way over here.
Where can I look for "Mama's Beads"? \nGive me a hint. Please.
Are you crazy? \nYou don't look for them, you need to cook them.
Well then, share the recipe.
Don't tell me what to do, \nand I won't tell you where to go... \nDon't you want to ask about the price of the issue?
Oh really! And this is a bosom buddy... \nI spit on you! With saliva!
E-eshkin cat! \nThis fucking elusive Sherstyuk is a pain in the ass!

I told you: \nyou can only catch him during the day outside the complex, if the mutants aren't acting up. \nAnd at night he sits and shoots back along with everyone else in the Research Institute building.
Well, I found the warrant officer. \nBut I didn't get anything out of him - he's not in the loop. \nWell, at least I traded vodka for grenades, so that's something.
Well, if a grandma had balls, \nshe would be a grandpa.
What's the point of saying it so beautifully?
You are our little dimwit... \nIf Sherstyuk knew everything, he wouldn't be a warrant officer, but a general!
I get it now... \nBut a plunderer of military property - \nis better than any general...
Three hundred Soviet wrappers for matches - \nis a completely realistic price in such places.
Bell-bottom jeans with rhinestones \nwill be cheaper in the Zone...
But matches are a necessity, \ntherefore the price is corresponding.
Near the Barrier, when I was returning to base, \nI found a glowing Gauss rifle - I can't pick it up. It's a shame...
I try to pick it up - \na small harmless "Greed" anomaly appears. \nThe toad is strangling me myself - I can't step away and I can't take it.
I see you from the tower through the optics. \nHow you're dancing in one place there. \nCome on, get your ass to base quickly!
But what about the Gauss rifle?
Ah, to hell with it! \nYou'll call the "Monolith" down on yourself...
If you were near - \nI'd drop it on your dick... Alright, I'm coming.
What kind of pepper showed up at your place?
They already told you? \nOne dude came here - looks like a hippie, \nbut otherwise he's alright, it's interesting to talk to him about this and that.
What do you mean like a hippie?
Well, he has this outfit. \nIn a suede vest, his hat is a cowboy one with this string... \nall covered in baubles, in round little glasses, long-haired, and he's also a pacifist, imagine that. :))
What do you mean, pacifist?
Walks around without a weapon. \nWe were shocked when he said he doesn't acknowledge weapons and doesn't want to take life away from the Zone... \nHe walks around completely empty. Just a knife. :)
Holy shit, I'm amazed! \nAnd nobody ate him while he was getting to you?
Well, he came with a guide, \nhired some stalker. He wanted to get closer to the center, the energy is greater here, you see...
He's generally on his own wavelength - \nwhen he came to us: \n"Hello people, I am Chapa, I'll live with you, is that okay?"
And that same evening he walked out of the base beyond the fence, \nstood there, spread his arms to the sides and closed his eyes...
Stood there for two hours. \nWe thought he was stoned. But then he told us, \nthat he was talking to the Zone like that. :)
And how did he talk? :))
Talked normally. \nThe next day he went somewhere and dragged back a "Flash", traded it with us for grub...
So, if he starts dragging them like that \nevery day, then we'll be eating chocolate marzipans now. :)
Whoa! The little guy is not simple... \nI should talk to him too. Maybe he'll put in a good word with the Zone for me. :)
Is there a way to look for mines?
And why the hell are you going into a minefield?
It's too late for a girl to ask her mom for permission, \nwhen she's no longer a girl. I'm already in a minefield.
Are you stoned, or what?
No way... \nDecided to shortcut the way to the Barrier. And it looks like I shortcut my life...
Alright, don't say goodbye ahead of time. \nA bolt - it's a stalker's best friend even in a minefield...
You won't blow up a mine with a bolt, \nbut on impact you can hear a characteristic click.
Okey-dokey! Got it. \nProceeding to throw.
Seen Mikha Karelsky recently?
He drops by sometimes. \nNot a frequent guest. Why?
Just because, have a couple of questions, \nin his line of work.
I'll whistle when he shows up. \nOr what should I ask?

Better I ask myself. 
He gave a tip last time. 
Need to clarify where and what. 
People got confused.
He can do that. \nHe'll twist the situation so much, \nthat there's no way you'll get it.
That's what I'm talking about. \nIn short, whistle, \nI'll gallop over in a flash.
Alright.
Hey. \nWhat did you want?
Hey. \nAren't you heading to the Radar?
I am. What?
Need to meet a person there, he's in the forest, \nhe has something for me.
Who is it?
Just an acquaintance. It doesn't matter. \nWill you take some swag from him for me?
No, it matters. Who is he? \nLoner? "Duty"?
No, not quite...
"Monolith"?! \nGo you know where!
Does it really matter to you? \nHe won't waste you, don't worry. \nThe main thing is that you don't waste him by accident.
No, I said - no. \nLukash will take my head off, what's wrong with you?!
Well, if you chatter \nleft and right - he will, of course.
No, don't ask. \nNot for any money.
Well, alright. \nThen let's do this: he makes a stash, you take it. \nHe'll drop you the coordinates, I gave him your contacts.
Are you a ram, or what?! \nWe might be being tailed!!! Text him right now, \nso he doesn't even think about contacting me!!!
And anyway - go to hell, \nI don't want to have any more business with you. \nMy life is dear to me. That's it, piss off!
Hey! \nKnow what the eggheads are saying about artifacts?
What do you mean?
Anyway, they say, \nyou can't keep them on you for a long time, some energy radiates.
Radiation? \nThat's been known for a long time.
Nah. Something else. \nYou can get severely mentally disturbed...
Like I heard about stalkers: \nthey were saying something about Burr and Twang too...
And our Vanka Six-fingered, remember? \nAnd this one, what's his name... the cross-eyed one.
Why are you mixing everyone together, \nwho knows who has what quirks. \nVanka in general, they say, kicked the bucket from an overdose...
And stalkers themselves are headless - \nthey climb wherever...
So what exactly is it, \nwhat exactly can't you keep for long?
Do I know? It's not that important to me, \nI don't keep them on me - I hand everything over or stash it. \nI thought you knew, you are our "black belt" :)
Nah, I haven't heard anything.
So zip over to the nerds at Yantar, \nfind out, then tell us. :)
Are you crazy? \nWho will let me in there?
Well, put the pressure on someone, \nwho's wandering around here with nothing to do.
We'll see.
Hey. Made up your mind or not?
I don't know... It's kinda scary. \nYou have your own rules there.
What rules? \nI also thought at first...
The first time I came like this, \nand the sentry at the entrance says to me: \n"Who are you? Hand over your gun or get out of here!"
And pokes his finger at me... \nPaws are dirty, he stinks himself.
And there's a sniper on the tower - \nyelling and shooting into the open air, whether at crows, \nor because he stuffed himself with pills. Well, I think, what a company.
There are sentries after all? \nMeans there's duty...
Nah, they are mostly there for show. \nAnd they themselves collect tribute from passers-by - with ammo and vodka.
And otherwise it costs these "sentries" nothing \nto leave their post to go get drunk somewhere.
So is there a senior or someone?
The whole beauty is that nobody specific. \nNot like the Duty guys - all sorts of quads with a senior, with a career ladder...
There is no such thing here that they order \nand you bleed from the nose to do it - not at all. \nThere is one dude - Lukash. He's kind of in charge here...
So he comes out, says: \n"Bros! We need to stand watch. You understand yourselves, \nthat all sorts of trash wanders around the area and could wander in here"...
"Dudes," he says, \n"I can also stand watch if you want."
And he stands at the post himself? \nYou're lying...
He would have stood... but the guys tell him: \n"Come on, dad! We'll stand watch"...
They themselves want to drink, \nand he's still kind of the commander, so you understand yourself, \nwhen any kind of brass is nearby, it just doesn't go down the hatch...
But generally, he's a great guy, \neverything is simple with him. Come on, you wanted to. \nNo obligations and any of that Duty bullshit.
I'm heading there right now. \nI'll come over, you'll tell me more about what's what.
Waiting... Grab a five-pack of bottles. \nIf you treat right away - the guys will accept you as their own. :))
Hello. \nIt's me - Viktor. \nI know you can receive me. Respond.
Are you on your own or "on an errand"?
Well, good boy... \nI won't hide it, I was asked. You don't make contact with anyone anymore. \nBut I wanted to talk to you myself.
About what? Does this still bother you? \nYou said that all of this is complete nonsense...
Well, I didn't quite say that. \nIt's just... well, agree, this "superman theory" of yours is a bit of a stretch. \nYour perfection. And for some reason it's specifically only yours...
And didn't you ever have the feeling, \nthat something was specifically predestined for you? \nThat your destiny - is to do something incredible?
Of course, I've had the feeling of my own importance, \nsignificance, responsibility... \nBut not super-uniqueness.
We are all unique actually.
But some are more unique, right? \nYou, for example... :))
Possibly. \nThis is natural selection. It has to start with one individual, \nwho seemingly at first looks ordinary...
But the genetic code is already in him, \nand it will lead him to the next step of evolution, \nif a leap is somehow triggered by a catalyst, helping him with something.
Listen, I just want to help you. \nDistract yourself from these thoughts. You haven't been yourself since \nyou found this artifact...
Well, why did you take it? \nYou stole the keys, opened the safe, told no one... It would have laid under lock and key, \nand you would be doing something more worthwhile than petty theft.
No. You don't understand... \nWhen evolution gives something, it sets conditions...
And it can ask you to do something, \nthat contradicts your being and the "common" sense of the crowd. \nIt might seem strange to those around... Someone might even start considering you crazy...

You guys probably already consider me as such. \nBut all this is unimportant, the only important thing is survival and perfection, \nand further perfection for the sake of survival now and in the future...
Alright, you probably \ndidn't quite understand us correctly. We actually all want to help you. \nWhere are you? Tell us, and we will come.
No!!! \nYou won't find me, I am leaving. And you won't take this from me!
"Your precious"? :)))
Don't laugh! \nYou really all understand nothing... None of you.
I thought everything was much better. :( \nDon't disappear, I'm asking you. Stay in touch.
That's it. \nDon't contact me anymore.
My dear sir, it is impossible to work like this! \nThere is absolutely no data, we are poking around like blind kittens, knowing nothing!
Ivan Sergeevich, \nwe are already going fucking crazy here!
Excuse me, what are you doing there? \nAm I really communicating with my postgraduate student right now, and not with some drunk stalker, no?..
Colleague! \nI hope you are simply fatigued, and haven't abused alcohol. You need to rest, I strongly advise you. \nBut the measurements are absolutely necessary for us, you must understand this.

Ivan Sergeevich, \nit must give you pleasure to be ironic towards a postgraduate student, and not a doctor of science at all... \nwhere would we be. Here, you know, it's not the department, adrenaline clears the brains very well indeed...
Or else come down to us, eh?! \nWe've been sitting here for days on end, dirty as pigs, \nadvancing science, lugging sensors on our humps, staring at anomalies.
I will ask you to lower your tone!.. \nFine, I am sending a relief shift. You definitely need rest now.
Colleague, \nyou looked at the latest results, didn't you. And what are the conclusions?
Ambiguous, colleague, ambiguous. \nThe variability of development forms, this incredible resistance to external stimuli...
And the very causes of their emergence \nmake you spend more than one sleepless night, don't they?
That is so. \nI remember, during the first analysis of the characteristics I couldn't come to my senses for a long time... \nPandora's box!
You know, some people actually \nhook these formations onto themselves and wear them. It gives you the creeps just thinking \nwhat this could lead to, but they are like little children.

Agreed. 
On the other hand, as cynical as it may sound, those very people, 
hooking all these new formations onto themselves, give us extremely interesting results 
regarding their effects on the human body. Volunteers of science.
They call these formations "artifacts". \nBy the way, their very behavior is also an extremely curious phenomenon...
Sinyavsky Anton Sergeevich, a sociopsychologist, \ndidn't leave the field bunkers in his time, but he's perhaps the only one \nwho sufficiently observed the people themselves...
So he is putting out \nsome extremely interesting hypotheses right now!
Curious.
His latest analysis \n"On Mental States..." echoes your note \non the somatic changes of synapses. Everything and everyone is subject to mutations.
What do you expect... \nEven materials undergo changes, a brick, for example, shows the density properties \nof wood, but the visible characteristics are all the same...
The weight is the same, but it floats! \nAnd here we have living tissue... God knows what!
Yes... Well, it was a pleasure talking. \nDuty calls.
Do you have insatiable curiosity, or some other goal?
We didn't finish talking... \nYou started talking about "chrono..." something there. Remember? And specifically - where?
Well, please, \nalthough you'll hardly find it interesting...
Let it be known to you, my dear fellow, \nthe entire Zone is a continuous anomaly, and it's not just that \nthe flora and fauna are represented quite bizarrely here...
It's that you and I, willy-nilly, \nfall under the influence of these completely unstudied fields and radiations.
Leave the empty talk. \nEven a freshman understands that. I'm talking about something else: the city of Pripyat.
And what, a most beautiful city, I'll tell you. \nI was there once. It still retains some charm even now...
We received information \nthat many stalkers have seen a lot of unusual things there. \nIn particular: they saw the movement of cars on the city streets...
Ordinary civilian cars and buses. \nNoticed the movements of people in ordinary clothes. \nHave you happened to hear anything like that?
Oh! If you sit with that brotherhood \nby a campfire in the evening, and put out a flask of pure alcohol to boot - \nyou'll hear even crazier things...
But it is noteworthy that those who observed all this \nunanimously claim the exact same thing: \nthe buildings, streets, squares at those moments looked quite new...
I believe that these phenomena \nare some kind of visualization of chronoclusters, time pockets, so to speak...
And therefore, my dearest fellow, \nif you happen to visit those places, be so kind \nas to tell me about any such incidents, alright?
You love your intricacies... \nAlright, I'll inform you if I find anything out, only getting there is a bit difficult, to put it mildly... :))
I am aware of that. \nAnd that makes it even more strange and incomprehensible... \nIf, of course, all these "eyewitnesses" can be believed.
You can use those informalities, comrade, \nwith your wife, if you have one.
Excuse me, where did I use informalities? \nA poor knowledge of Russian word forms is not a virtue.
Leave your dirty tricks \nof casuistry alone. Better tell me, \nwhy do the measurements once again correspond very approximately to the points specified in the assignment?
It's simply because the executors are fundamentally afraid \nto climb into that hellhole. Do you yourself even have an idea \nof where you're forcing them to place the scanners?
I fully understand. \nAny stalker rookie would get through there...
But yours are afraid to singe their asses a little bit. \nIf you can't force people - go yourself. \nStop eating the government's bread for nothing!
You just can't get through there! \nThey tried more than once.
Into the field, my young friend, into the field. \nAnd I want the results of precise measurements on my desk by Monday!
I would have smeared that guard's brains \nafter the first such game \nall over Sidor's bunker, and my hand wouldn't have flinched.
Have you blown out many brains already? \nWith an unflinching hand.
Alexey Nikolaevich, \nall three snorks died shortly after turning on the beta generation apparatus. \nRadiation parameters: intensity - 30, reflection coefficient - 0.012...

Unfortunately, their death \ncould not be detected in time, so the remains, having lain under radiation for a long time, \nacquired an appearance not conducive to further research of the corpse as such.
Understood. But I think that's not the main thing. \nWhat else happened?

During careless handling of the remains \nKolotaev suffered acute intoxication, which led to a general affection of his body's skin cover. \nI request permission for the final removal of Kolotaev from the working group.

Viktor Andreevich, \neven when you were a student you were famous for your ability to hide your mistakes behind bureaucratic phrasing. \nDo you think I don't understand why you are writing allegorically on a secure channel?..
Hoping that your tricks will work on me too? \nSo tell me, where was Zelensky when you and Kolotaev, having turned on the beta apparatus, went off to drink?
Why immediately drinking? \nIt's even offensive to hear. We were working...
They were working... \nAnd where were the rest of the lab assistants?..

The fact that none of you discovered \nthe death of the test subjects indicates that you approached the work carelessly. \nAnd why could you "not detect in time" the death of the subjects "after some time"? I know why!
Well, why then?
Simply because you, \ninstead of setting the intensity to 30, set it to 60 with your drunk eyes. And went off to drink! Or to cure a hangover! \nI simply don't know exactly when your snorks croaked.
Alexey Nikolaevich, \nit wasn't like that at all...
You're lying, and very brazenly at that! \nThe intensity cannot be 30, and the RC - 0.012, they taught you that in your third year!
Such an RC only happens at an intensity \nfrom 58 to 63, it's easy to calculate in your head! Is Kolotaev also a corpse? \nAm I correctly understanding "final removal from the group"?..
Until my return, leave everything as it is, \nperform no work, compile a full and HONEST Report. \nWhen I return - someone will be "removed from the group"! Finally!
Mikhail Vasilievich, \nI want to inform you that starting tomorrow you are taking up guard duty. \nIt's a 24-hour shift, once a week, starting at 20:00.
And why is that? \nI was told we wouldn't be involved in this. \nI already got ready to start the experiments... who do I leave it to?
I know your experiments, they can wait... \nDon't worry, you're only there to help at the simplest post. \nOur security is short on men.

Then write to wherever you need to, \nlet them send a whole platoon. Well, this is ridiculous! I'll be chilling on the territory now, \ngoofing off all day long, and then you'll be asking where the results are!

Stop arguing already! 
I understand everything and I will give you an extra day to complete the experiment. 
That's it! 
Yes, get some sleep beforehand. You will receive weapons and gear from me.
Howdy! \nWill you be ordering equipment tomorrow?
Well. We planned to. \nThough our requests aren't ready yet. \nNeed to justify it properly - they're painfully stingy on the mainland...
Write in two suits there. \nBut normal ones, orange peel is not valued on the Radar.
What orange peel?
The orange protective suit. Don't be dense.
And why don't you write your own request?
What do you mean my own? \nWho do you sign your request with? With Sakharov. \nAnd we are with Sakharov. So what's the difference?
But our funds are being spent!

For some reason you guys over there decided, \nthat if a group is sent to the Radar, then their equipment should be supplied somehow differently. \nWho are we going to sign with here? With the local zombies? With the pseudodogs?
Alright, let's drop the subject. \nWhy are you all so nervous over there? After talking to you, you walk around twitchy all day. \nMake the list, toss it to me, I'll give it to Sakharov to sign.
Why don't you come over here, \nfor at least a week - we'll see how your nerves are doing. \nSitting there in the bunker, sipping tea...
Well, here we go... \nAlright, that's it. Toss the list.
Alexander Leonidovich, \nwhat on earth is going on over there with you?
What?
What? Did you send your postgraduate students to the object? \nAnd what did they pull off there? It's already been reported to me...
Those aren't ours. Mine are all sitting on their benches, \nwaiting for the command, you yourself said that the examination would be comprehensive...
Did I really tell you that? \nRight, I'll be there with you personally soon and I'll explain everything to you there...
For God's sake... \nFirst one thing, then another. I'm sick of it already.
Did something happen? Any news?
Basically, I'm giving you the gathered information \nwith a preliminary analysis. So...
