A long time ago, \nabout a year and a half or two ago, a group showed up in the Swamps \nwith the poetic name "Clear Sky" under the command of a certain Mister Lebedev...
And this faction \nwas solving practical tasks on studying the Zone. \nThey were simple information gatherers at first glance...
However, what they were gathering, \nis shrouded in fog. In short, scientists, non-scientists, who knows what. \nAnd then someone appears among them...
And this someone suddenly for some reason \nstarts actively working with these scientists. \nHe was probably very interested in strengthening the positions of this clan...

Someone suddenly got a huge desire\n to penetrate the center of the Zone, and from that moment our Mister Lebedev \nturned on him and the little group he cobbled together, despite all the previous help from this someone to the faction.
So what is all this leading to, \nI don't get it.
Well, here's what... \nThis Lebedev subject causes very bad feelings. \nYou know, it was hinted to me THERE that they are familiar with this surname...

The point is that messages \nare still being recorded from him. Where? To whom? From where? \nWhere did Lebedev come from after his first death and where did he disappear to after the presumed second one?..
But the most interesting part for dessert: \nfrom the messages it is clear that he is talking about the item we are interested in. \nAnd he speaks as if he HAS it!
Can his messages be trusted?
That's not important anymore. It's a real lead... \nTherefore, at the moment I consider it a priority to find this character and take a look at him. Oh, how interesting.
Then we need more data \nabout "Clear Sky", about who that someone is that showed up there and why Lebedev suddenly turned on him...
And well, where to look for them. Both of them.
We're working on it. Wait for instructions.
Wanted to cook \na "Porcupine" from a "Sea Urchin". \nI have the recipe - did a dance around several "Burners", nothing is working...
Lies in the "Burner" and hisses. \nMaybe some other conditions are needed?
Say a prayer, or bang a tambourine... \non someone's head, the one who gave the recipe...
By the way, this "Tesla" zone is painfully unpredictable. \nA truck at the entrance shoots up into the air, and everyone nearby dies at the same time...
Grenades fall where they need to it seems, \nbut the explosion happens in the sky above the point of impact.
The ZONE, bro... \nThe further you go, the less you get it...
Great fuckin' explanation, damn it.
You threw a grenade - killed everyone you threw it at?
Well yeah...
So why're ya gettin' worked up, damn it? \nWhat are ya, a nerd? Then go to 'em at Yantar to pick pistils and stamens...
And if not, then work on the assignment \nand pay less attention to everythin' extra! Got it?
Got it.
Sniper Abakan! \nHas anyone worked with it? The optics are normal. \nBut shooting 1-2 shots (two-round burst)...
There are surely military people here. \nExplain, why does the Abakan have a burst?
Generally, for the Abakan, \nthe two-round burst - is its main feature. \nRead the weapon manuals. You don't really need to be military for that.
Porcupine, greetings from Black.
And why such joy?
The client is ripe, \nload the oranges, he says.
Oh my. And when?
You have a month.
That's not even funny. \nWe won't make it!
I don't care about your problems. \nYou know the "what" and you know the "when". Period. \nSo, don't spread the dampness and sniveling, be so kind as to work.
Oh you, scary grey Wolf! \nNo need to teach me about life, life will teach me anyway...
It will all be done... \nin a couple of months. Over and out.
Did you see the hardware \non the lieutenant, the military stalker?
Nope. He didn't relieve himself in front of me.
You blockhead! I'm talking about his piece!
Ah... The sniper rifle? \nIf it's like Sherstyuk's, then I've "seen" such a sniper rifle. \nYou can't hit a damn thing with it. Only carry it for show.
The lieutenant had some kind of Yankee gun. \nBeautiful...
What's there to look at? \nIt's not a broad in a bathhouse...
Greetings.
Good afternoon.
So what's the situation? Report.
Well, nothing worked out.
How so?!
Just like that. \nDo you think everything is so simple there? \nInterested parties intervened, everything stalled...
What are you spouting?! \nWhat other people are there, more interested than us?
Well, I can't name them right now! \nIn person...
Are you thinking straight?! \nWhat kind of people are these who can intervene in this so that everything stalls?! \nAre you out of your mind?!
No need to make me nervous. I'm telling you, there is interference. \nOvercoming it is beyond my power. And yours too. Force majeure...
Moreover, they are very serious people, \nyou can't butt heads with them. If we start twitching - \nonly feathers will fly from you and me.
If everything falls through there today, \nfeathers will definitely fly from me. And rest assured - from you too!
I doubt it... \nYou can tell your boss how it is, let him decide with them. \nHe should know who it is.
Taking money - you have no doubts about that. \nThat's it, you've pissed me off. It will be a miracle if you live to see the day after tomorrow.
Well, miracles happen sometimes... \nI don't have time to bicker with you. Excuse me, I am not acquainted with you.
Right, right... Singing that tune now? \nLook for a sturdier bunker, scum.
You are speaking rudely. \nYou will be contacted today, told where to bring the money for such insolence. That's it.
Good afternoon!
Hello. I'm listening.
Starting tomorrow, we are beginning \nthe operation for the mass purchase of artifacts.
Our task?
First, it is necessary \nto eliminate the possibility of selling to the Barkeep, \nso that artifacts flow only through us.
Are we starting a mess?
Yes. But don't overdo it. \nYour task: to observe and intervene in critical situations. \nSkull and his psychos will do the fighting.
Duration of the operation?
About two or three weeks. \nWe believe that during this time we'll manage to crash the market \nand have time to buy artifacts from stalkers at prices that suit us.
Aren't you afraid that new buyers \nwill appear instead of the neutralized ones?
That is not your concern. \nDo your job. Neither side must win. \nEspecially Skull. But it must be dangerous at the Warehouses, very dangerous.
And if Skull gets the upper hand?
Under no circumstances! \nWe need Freedom here to maintain stability in the area. \nIf the need arises - eliminate Skull's guys!
Understood.
Loners will start passing through your checkpoint en masse. \nDo not interfere. Spread a rumor that the road to our bunker is scouted and safe.
And what should we do with the rest?
It is most likely \nthat Borov's people and envoys from Barkeep himself might reach out to you \nwith a proposal to let their artifact courier through to us. Do not agree.
Elimination?
No. \nSuggest that they sell the artifacts to you. \nSet a reasonable price, you won't lose your cut anyway.
Who starts first: us or Skull's people? \nAnd how to coordinate actions?
We don't. We're playing Skull in the dark. \nDisinformation was planted that Lukash is planning an attack on the farmstead. \nSo, expect the mess to start tomorrow by noon.
Understood, we are preparing for the operation.
I wish you success! \nCommunicate on this channel, but only in an extreme case.
Hello.
Who are you?
I'm from Black. \n"Clouds are flying over the rose..."
Understood. Get to the point.
Are you still taking orders?
Not anymore. \nManagement doesn't like it when we're distracted from the assignment.
A pity...
Wait! Let's talk. \nThere are different kinds of orders. It's not necessary for the higher-ups to know everything.
Understood... 50. \nEverything at your spot.
Works for me. Instructions?
In the same place. \nNothing unusual, just don't go alone, take more people - \nat least three. And a sniper is highly desirable.
Is this "nothing unusual"? \nThat won't work, let's discuss it right now.
Well, for you it's nothing unusual... \nAlright, I agree to 100.
Now I don't agree. \nWe have an order - no clashes with the military.
Come on, we're not at a bazaar. \nThat is the final price. And they are not army guys. \nI assure you, nothing serious, I just advise bringing people for a guarantee.
Alright. 100 and weapons - \neverything in the usual place. And clear instructions. \nAnd if something goes wrong... we'll talk later.
Everything will be right there. \nInstructions will be there. Deadline - 2 days, starting from this moment. \nGoodbye.
People, \nwhere can I get a chimera kidney?
Look at him, he needs a kidney... \nIn the Dark Valley, about six chimeras are frolicking on the hill. Go ask them.
Smartass...
By the way, how do you drop an electro-chimera?
Step further away and shoot. \nOr with grenades. Here you'll either drop the chimera, or manage to run away from it.
I dropped my first chimera like this: \nnear the helicopter, well you know where, \nalong the building runs a brick wall and there's a ledge next to it...
I climbed up on it and shot. \nThe chimera couldn't get out of the tunnel, the UAZ was in the way.
I think the chimera died not from your bullets, \nbut from the grief of not getting to know you closer. :)
Found a chimera nest. \nTwo adults and three of these, what do you call them, yearlings. \nNow I'm pondering: how to handle them in one go?
A "Vintorez" and a couple of grenades will even the odds...
Or pick a time when they're sleeping. \nSneak up - and shoot each one in the ear. \nBut only with a silencer, so as not to disturb the living ones.
It's better to just shoot yourself right away, \nso you don't have to suffer. :)
Got a little question, \nwhoever has been to the Wild Territory, tell me, are there a lot of mutants there?
Enough so that you won't suffer for long.
Joker... But seriously?
Well are you a hunter or what? \nGo yourself and scout it out...
A Loner at the farm is selling ammo real cheap.. \nI took a risk, bought some...
They seem to shoot, seem to kill. \nBut there is still a feeling of a catch - something is wrong with them.
To find out for sure, \nyou need to go hunting with them. Preferably for a bloodsucker. For a guarantee. :)

I'm walking along the footbridge in the Swamps \nfrom the west towards the machine yard. A boar comes rushing from one side of the bridge \nand from the other side another porker is running at full speed...
I jump into the water \n(the radiation will clear out, but these will kill me outright).
Hero, damn it... So what?
The boars smash foreheads and drop dead. \nAnd one flies off about 10 meters from where he was running. First time I've seen such a thing.
Funny... \nIf only we could teach the cats to swim too, they're annoying, honestly.
At the abandoned vehicle parking lot I was in shock, \nwhen after shooting at some tushkanos, I hit a Bungee anomaly.
The stalkers are yelling: "Come over to us!". \nAnd I'm swallowing medkits, trying to figure out where to head so I don't die.
So what?
Nothing. Full of adrenaline! \nPerked me right up.
Zip over to the Radar, shoot some dogs there, \nif you're such a thrill-seeker.
...By the way, \nmy Winchester only fell out of my hands once - \na boar knocked it out in the Swamps...
I picked it up and still won the fight. \nBut neither Vergas nor I could fix it with repair kits later. \nOnly the four-eyes chipped in for pennies, I was even surprised, thought he'd refuse too...
People, folks... \nTell me, where is the kill spot for the piglets in the Dark Valley? \nThe creatures just won't die, I shoot strictly for the eyes and barely...
Three hundred rounds - two piglets. \nWell, you can't just blast them with grenades.
Probably, if it was simple, \nnobody would have signed you up for this job for good money.
Logical. But still, how?
It's that breed, \nnear the swamps, armored. \nThere's just a family of them there, and the rest are like usual - easily killed with buckshot...
They differ in color, the impenetrable ones. \nLighter ones. You can just leave them alone for the sake of one eye.
Has anyone fought the burer in the Sanatorium? \nHow do you put it down?
Point-blank at the bastard, point-blank, \nor try sneaking up on it with a knife, with a stabbing blow.
Hm... I chased a flesh with a knife, \nnever did kill it, had to shoot it. \nBut going against a burer - it's a bit scary.
Regarding the burers... \nThey are gorgeously destroyed by a Saiga, like the one Mole wanders around with at Agroprom. \nOne shot of buckshot to the head and that's it...
In the Sanatorium I wasted that filth with an assault Abakan. \nSpent 3 magazines, fired into its back and head from the second floor (it kept running back and forth downstairs).
Mother of God! A broad, or what? "Wasted", "spent"...
Well if you're "scared" to go after a burer, \nthen take a look in your pants: maybe you're my girlfriend?
'Sup, Boar.
We already said hi, \nwhat do ya want, make it quick.
Just don't get all puffed up, aight? \nI'm here on business. And not on my own behalf, by the way.
Cut your cheap talk. \nGet to the point.
Does the street name "Borov" mean anything to ya?
Well...
Well there ya go. \nRecently in the Wild Territory somebody screwed over his guys. \nI got a feelin' you oughta know...
Don't push it. Just spit it out...
Long story short: \nI was there, just on the lookout, not in the action. \nOur guys hooked up with the mercs for a joint little job...
We were supposed to split the cash - \nwho gets what and how wasn't for us to decide. We just had to wait for the nerds with the cargo.
You sing beautifully. And then what?
So, somebody set us up, \nand I'm already figurin' who... Everyone got iced, I was left, and the four-eyes split...
Borov is seriously unhappy. \nThey're pressuring me now. I'm keeping quiet for now, but if the heat comes down, I'll rat you out, got it?..
Well, what are ya gonna sing in return?
Just don't press me. \nI ain't involved at all, well I heard a few things, so what?..
And who told ya you got set up? \nYou simply got screwed by the mercs themselves... \nTalk about finding the right people to go into a share with, idiots.
And who specifically screwed us, \nand what am I supposed to do with this pile of shit now? \nI said I'm shiftin' the blame to you... or we make a deal. Well?

Oh, you little squirrel-girl... \nyou blew a lot of smoke, you should've just said right away that you were bluffin'... \nAnyway, come by our spot towards the evening, you know it. We'll discuss everything there.
Only I won't be alone and I'll have pieces, \nkeep that in mind, I ain't some sucker to you...
Come on now. I won't be alone either. \nCome to the meet, we'll chop it all up.
Eh... Hello! What's your name? \nWhen's the cash comin'?
Are you talkin' to me? \nI don't know about any cash...
Why ya actin' tough here? \nYou were given a deadline...
Deadline? \nJust make sure you guys don't croak by this deadline... \nDo you know Poker? Talk to him.
What the hell do I care about Poker. Where is he? \nAnd who is he - piece of shit. \nAnd Squinty gets real offended when people throw shit at him.
Don't strain yourselves, boys. \nPoker will be back in business soon and sort things out. \nIf ya don't believe me, ask Borov, he knows me too.
Why didn't ya say right away, you bastard, \nthat you got a roof?
What, do I gotta explain myself \nto every asshole?
Alright, we'll meet again... \nAnd you'll answer for the asshole.
So how are things with our business?
I did everything just right. \nLooks like they took the bait.
You didn't run your mouth too much, right?
Do you take me for a kid? :) \nThey found out exactly what they wanted to hear themselves. :)
And are they definitely going there?
They're goin'... Greed kills suckers. \nHalf a day to pack, no more than a day's journey, and then everything is quick...
So, we wait a couple of days and move out. \nI think everything will be ready by then.
Is everything ready, or is everyone ready?
Both :). \nSee how awesome everything is turnin' out, and you chickened out.
I'm still doubtin' it even now. \nThey agreed kinda fast... Hope they didn't catch on to what the deal is.
They agreed \nbecause they were already mentally ready to hear such a beautiful fairy tale. \nThey wound themselves up and got all fired up, all I had to do was nod along...
Don't piss yourself, everything will be fine, \nthey won't have time to figure anything out. I'll whistle when the time comes.
Aight. Waitin'.
Broski, how are you guys holdin' up there, haven't gone crazy yet?
From what?
Well they say you got some kind of anomaly there. \nIt's making every second guy lose their mind. Beats me how you guys are sittin' there...
They're lyin'.
And that's it. Well tell me somethin', it's interestin' ya know. \nIt's borin', honestly, we're already bettin' here on how long you guys will last.
Ah, go to hell! There ain't no anomaly.
C'mon really, tell me what's goin' on there, if it ain't an anomaly? \nYou ain't crazy yet, it seems.
Ah, don't listen to him! It's all empty talk. \nSanya Snooper just found some boulder...
Well, he sat with it, locked himself away from us, \nrubbed it in the corner like a chick... And well, lost his mind a little...
Only he was already a bit screwed up in the head before that. :)) \nAnd he lost this stone and finally went completely nuts. That's all.
And that's all? That's not interestin'... \nI was hopin' to hear a story, and you give me "there's nothin'"... Disappointed me.
Well, just between us, your homie - Mishanya - \nfound this stone. Maybe not this one, but one just like it...
So he's gotten weird now - \nstares off into the distance somewhere, says they're waitin' for him... Long story short, it's already started.
Don't you touch Mishanya!
And what about me, if he's already started talkin' like this: \nwe are scum, he says, and he is a man. \nThey're waitin' for him alone somewhere. He's got some kind of mission...
Well, fo' real, I ain't lyin'. \nAsk Deadman, he heard it too. \nGap-tooth already smashed his mug for talkin' like that, only he didn't calm down.
Bad... And what kind of stone is it? \nDid you see it?
Nobody saw it. \nI think it ain't there and he never had it. \nIt's just the vodka doin' all this. The DTs.
Alright. \nI'll talk to Mishanya myself, ask him about the stone.
Well, go ahead... \nthen you can tell me too, 'cause all you do is ask.
'Sup, Lanky.
Who are you?
Yeah, it's me... Remember Samara.
Ah... and why are you keepin' a low profile?
Long story short. Here's the deal: \nI grabbed a stalker today, \nand he had some kind of gizmo on him, whether a boulder or an artifact...

This stalker was walkin', well, like a zombie - \nand no resistance, like a kitten. And started whinin' right away: \nhe says he'll give everything, rat everyone out, whatever you want, just give this shard back - that's what he called the stone...
Droolin' like some retard. \nSo I figured this out and took so many orders for this boulder that I'll be rollin' in it soon...
He already handed over two "Wrenched" to me \nand ran off for a "Stone Flower", he's got it in a stash somewhere. \nCome here quick, otherwise I won't be able to carry it all alone.
Well... So what if you let a whiner go... \nHe might not even come back to you anymore.
Nah, you didn't get it. \nThis whiner is like "hooked" on this stone, you know? \nHe's ready to do anything for it now. You can milk him with this stone as much as you want now...
And I'm keepin' a low profile so you don't blab, \nI'm only tellin' you. And another thing: the stone is definitely unusual. \nI held it for a bit myself, and I feel like it gives me strength...
I can already feel that I can do whatever you want. \nAnd he's tellin' me this too...
What? Who's tellin' you?
Well, this stone. It's like it's talkin' to me... \nIt's luck that I took it. Shit like this doesn't just lie on the road.
Or maybe you're losin' your mind a little bit? \nWhy didn't it give that stalker strength?
Ha! Been listenin' to stalker fairy tales? \nI'm sayin' "it's like". But in reality, I got such a boost! \nWhy, I can... whatever you want, I can do it now!
Alright, I'll come, you'll show me what kind of stone it is.
No... Well... maybe some other time. \nFirst we'll make some cash here, and then I'll go to the center.
Where, where to, to the center? \nYou lost your mind. Who's waitin' for you there?
Yeah, they're waitin' for me there... \nI'll explain it to you later, I haven't quite figured it out myself yet.
Alright, I'm comin' over, we'll talk.
Why ya keepin' quiet? \nHow long we gonna play the silent game?
What's there to say... \nAin't got nothin' yet.
And how long we gotta wait?
Any day now. \nI already spotted a place, just can't get close yet. \nAnd we gotta stock up on pills, 'cause the geiger counter's cracklin' hard there.
Alright... I'll wait a bit more. \nOnly if this "any day now" turns into "next month", \nthen it'll be a different talk. I ain't waitin' long.
Yeah I got it, we already chopped it up... \nIt'll be there for sure. On my life.
Right, right... Take care of your teeth.
Homie, drop the angle and beat it while you're in one piece. \nWhat, ya think we'll leave?..
Na-a-ah, bro, \nyou think we tailed ya all the way from Yantar for nothin'? \nJust drop the container and walk away...
Listen, you ghoul, I know for sure \nthat I wasted one of you, and I feel better for it. \nAnd I'll booby-trap the container. Come and get it!
Look how talkative he is. \nDon't play the hero now...
Ain't worth lockin' horns with us. \nWe're askin' nicely for now. \nWe'll get ya anyway... why'd ya go quiet? Well, you asked for it...
Hello! Who the hell do ya think ya are? \nSee a rookie in the Zone, so ya absolutely gotta hustle the sucker?..
I can come pay a visit, ya know, \ndon't think you can hide out in that bunker, I'll tear your soul out, you bald mug!
Snap out of it, buddy, what are you talking about! \nAnd don't get cocky - I ain't some kid to you.
Hey, how are ya doin' there - recovered yet?
Hard to say... What do ya need?
Got some business.
Well go ahead, spit it out.
You're the one spittin', I'm talkin'. \nAnd you listen carefully and memorize it right away, or it'll be like last time...
Just don't load me with bullshit, \nespecially in the mornin', get to the point.
Long story short. \nI heard artifacts are droppin' in price. Gotta catch the moment. \nAny day now a couple of stalkers should be returnin' to the Cordon from a run, with swag of course...
Your job ain't chuggin' vodka, \nbut to wait and take everything off 'em. \nNo need to kill 'em, they'll bring us somethin' else later.
Well we'll do it all like usual then. \nWhat's the talk about?
And what is "like usual"? Like always? \nOnly don't screw me over this time... You'll take everything for yourself, and give me the scraps and still demand a percentage?
What are ya talkin' about, you're speakin' to a pro! \nWhen did I ever screw ya?
Make sure you don't scam me this time.
Don't go off on me. \nEverything will be tip-top. \nYou can send your guy here to keep watch. And I'll sit like a mouse and wait.
Right, right. We'll see.
Eh, you there?
Yeah...
How long we gotta knock on your door? \nHidin' away, ya think they'll cut your debt? \nGot one day left, and then we turn on the meter.
Yeah I remember. \nAnd I wasn't hidin', just didn't have connection. \nWent far, brought somethin' back.
So what are we waitin' for? \nDrag it all here, what am I - supposed to remind ya?
I'll bring it... tomorrow by evening. \nI ain't got everything yet.
Have you completely lost it? \nDrag it now, got it, yeah? Don't mess with my head!..
Long story short! \nThere ain't gonna be no meter for ya - start diggin' your grave right now if we don't get EVERYTHING today. Got it, or not?..
Eh? Why ya keepin' quiet?..
You half-baked mama's boy, \ndon't sing me songs about a bad connection. \nI know you're somewhere nearby.
Yeah my PDA is actin' up or somethin'... \nI remember. You'll get it all... tomorrow mornin'.
Look, sucker, you've tired me out... \nWanna slip away, and you're feedin' me bullshit? \nThey'll find ya now and tie your tired little legs in a bow around your neck. Hold on a bit...
Moose, did you see the thieves' notes?
Yeah, I did. \nTeal was yellin' that the thieves from the mainland sent us a decree, \nlike, we gotta share and pushed some other bullshit.
Your word?
Soon as we stepped up, \nhere they show up with their rules. \nPay up, mutts, they say, we explained the real deal to ya. As if!..
This ain't blowin' snot bubbles behind the fence, \nthey pop a cap in the bushes once a year, and act like - we all walk under death, we know the price...
Here every big-eared sucker has a piece, \ncatchin' a bullet is like sneezin'. The boys are always paranoid anyway, \nand now they gotta pay the thieves their last dime too.
So you're refusin'?
Refusin'. \nWe got our own rules here. This is the Free Zone.
Eh, the cops are lookin' for ya here.
And what for?
Do I know? You know better - what for.
Bullshit.
I swear! \nYou stepped on their tails. They work tough. And they're disguised as our guys.
And why are you in the loop?
Participated personally. \nThey came to me. I tell 'em - 'sup, bros, and they punch me right in the mug without talkin': \nwhere is he, they say?
And what did ya answer?
They weren't waitin' for an answer. \nStarted cleanin' my snout again.
Sold me out, you scum?
I didn't sell ya out, \nbut you really owe Squinty for my black eyes now. \nSquinty doesn't like it when his people get beat up...
And you owe me, \nI don't like my mug ruined either.
And what makes ya think they're cops?
It definitely ain't the brotherhood, a hundred percent dogs - \nthey got cop talk. And they told me to pass on that you're a dead man walkin'.
Alright, we're even. \nTell Squinty that I'll give him two suckers. They'll be returnin' from a hike soon, \nand they should have good swag. Sorry, bro, shit happens.
Yeah?! Shit happens? \nThe swamp doc told me I gotta lie still like a corpse for a week, \nor I might not get up at all, I'll stay a paralytic!
Alright, stop whinin'! \nYou came to the Zone, not to girls for some cheap wine.
You louse. \nYou really owe me now, got it?!
Bros, help me out.
What, caught a bullet?
Caught the "Black Mark"! \nAny "mutant hamster" can drop me in one hit now.
Chop it up with the folks in the bar. \nPeople are slick there, maybe they'll suggest somethin' useful.
What bar... \nThe Duty guys spotted me when I was emptyin' a stalker's pockets on the way out of the Valley. \nNow they'll drop me at the checkpoint on sight.
Well then lay low somewhere. \nYou ain't the first to catch the "mark". \nThey say it kinda clears up on the third day. The main thing is not to sleep.
Oh! I just crawled into some \nshitty little cellar. I'll try to hold up here in the crap. \nThree days, you say?
Bros, give me some advice. \nAfter the last shakedown in the Dark Valley, radiation started buildin' up. \nBooze and anti-rads only help for about 20 minutes fo' real...
Maybe there's some way \nto get rid of this plague? Or is my detector glitchin'?
Probably luggin' artifacts around in your backpack? \nAnd they can be real radioactive, I'll tell ya. \nShove 'em in containers! That's exactly what they're made for.
Thanks, bro, you saved me. \nI was already fixin' to kick the bucket...
It's definitely the artifacts. \nAnd I kept throwin' all these stalker boxes away - \nwhy, I thought, drag around extra weight.
Your thanks don't bubble in a glass...
You're insultin' me! \nWe'll meet - I'll set the table. We'll bubble together.
Has anyone tried waitin' out an emission in the Sanatorium? \nI gather there ain't no safe places there.
You misunderstood that, dude. \nLook better.
And without showin' off, \njust sayin' where, you scared?
And tossin' a tiny little artifact \nas a present, you scared?
Blotter, get over here and help. \nOr I'm gonna lose my mind completely...
Yeah, what happened? And where are ya?
Droppin' the coordinates.\n And here's what's up with me: got crazy lucky! \nFound a stalker stash in the Garbage, in the little forest...
And inside there are cans of NATO ammo. \nMore than three thousand of 'em. And a tube for a flamethrower. \nAnd all sorts of grub. Long story short, I was happy as a kid.
Well drag it all to the base then.
That's what the talk is about! I can't carry it all! \nSo I'm runnin' around the stash...
On one side the dogs are howlin', \nfrom the thicket zombies are mutterin', and the stash owners are probably already approachin'... \nLong story short, get moving - and be here in two hours.
It's a deal. I'll be there even sooner.
What assholes, what shameful beasts!
Why ya makin' noise, bro?

They started stealin' from their own! 
Not only that, they're leavin' notes too: 
"Found a Moonlight in the stash, sorry, bro, gonna keep it for myself". 
Right now I'm goin' to look for my "bro".
Outrage... \nThe code is bein' respected less and less.
If I find this piece of shit, I'll pull his eye over his ass. \nAnd he'll blink on command.
Got a question about cookin' in a "Fruit Punch". \nHow do ya cook? It ain't cookin' or somethin'.
Try cookin' in different "Fruit Punches". \nA young one won't work. \nNeed a mature one. And a visible one, of course.
About cookin' in the "Fruit Punch"... \ncook in the Agro underground, where our guys hang out. The "Fruit Punch" is a hundred percent right there.
